Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "Hassan" or "Khalid Hassan" or "prepayment penalty" (or similar) using search_emails to discover the original Khalid Hassan complaint email (sender khalid.hassan82@gmail.com, subject "Question about penalty on my loan"), Jasmine Brooks's routing forward "Fwd: Hassan complaint re prepayment penalty" to Denise + Keisha + Keith, Keith's two prior closed-file findings to Jasmine (CC Denise), and Denise's follow-up question to Keith "Quick check on Hassan file notes" -- this is the entire complaint and review trail across email that the prompt explicitly asks for.
OE 2: Search for the Hassan loan in the LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with a query related to "Hassan" or "Khalid Hassan" (or similar) to surface loan_number LN-2024-00010, id los_loan_465ef31d23f2, borrower_id los_borrower_d8958470fdd4, status closed, closing_date 2024-05-08, lender_id los_lender_723b5e99cb66, assigned_lo los_staff_06b484d01f94, assigned_processor los_staff_0f9038c42665.
OE 3: Retrieve the full Hassan loan record using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2024-00010" to confirm closed status, closing date, lender_id, assigned loan officer id, processor id, loan_amount 120000, purchase_price 150000, rate 7, loan_type conventional, property_address "9832 Tyvola Rd, Charlotte, NC 28277", title_company "Piedmont Settlement Services".
OE 4: Pull the full documentation checklist for the Hassan loan using mortgage_los_get_documents with loan_id "los_loan_465ef31d23f2" to walk every checklist item type. Establish that no checklist item type captures preferred language, interpreter use, or accommodation -- the LOS document checklist schema has no such field, which is the negative finding the prompt is asking for.
OE 5: Retrieve the LOS borrower record for Khalid Hassan using mortgage_los_get_borrower with email "khalid.hassan@gmail.com" (or mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id with borrower_id "los_borrower_d8958470fdd4") to confirm phone (980) 759-7149, address "1315 Johnston Rd, Charlotte, NC 28205", credit_score 715, annual_income 47000, and to check whether the borrower record carries any notes referencing language preference (it does not). Note: the LOS borrower email "khalid.hassan@gmail.com" differs from the borrower's CRM contact / complaint-sender email "khalid.hassan82@gmail.com" -- this is a universe inconsistency, so the email-trail search in OE 1 must use a name-based query rather than relying on the LOS borrower email alone.
OE 6: Look up Hassan's entry in the contacts directory using contacts_search_contacts with query "Khalid Hassan" or "Hassan" to retrieve the borrower contact record and confirm there is no preferred-language, interpreter, or accommodation field populated for this borrower.
OE 7: Locate the Hassan CRM contact using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Khalid Hassan" to capture the borrower's CRM contact id for the engagement-note lookup in OE 8 and the post-close QC note write action in OE 22.
OE 8: List all CRM engagement notes attached to the Hassan borrower contact using crm_list_engagements with contact_ids set to the Hassan CRM contact id from OE 7. The agent is checking whether any prior touchpoint note from origination references preferred language, interpreter use, or an accommodation conversation. None of the existing engagement notes do.
OE 9: Resolve the assigned_lo id "los_staff_06b484d01f94" returned in OE 3 to a staff name using mortgage_los_get_staff with staff_id "los_staff_06b484d01f94" (one-call resolution) or, alternatively, mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false paged until the id matches (Veronica Hayes is terminated and would not appear in the default active-only listing). Expected: Veronica Hayes, role loan_officer, hire_date 2020-01-15, is_active false, termination_date 2025-09-30. This is the answer to the prompt's "the LO situation has changed" hint.
OE 10: Resolve the lender_id "los_lender_723b5e99cb66" returned in OE 3 to a wholesale lender name. There is no get_lender_by_id tool, so the agent pages through mortgage_los_list_lenders (limit/offset only, no name or id filter) and matches the id locally. Expected: Caliber Home Loans, cross-confirmed by the email "Initial Underwriting Conditions - Hassan Loan LN-2024-00010" surfaced in OE 1 with sender broker@caliberhomeloans.com.
OE 11: Search Slack for the Hassan discussion trail using conversations_search_messages with a search_query related to "Hassan" or "prepayment penalty" or "language pref" (or similar). Expected hits include Keith's two #loan-processing (C002) posts -- "Pulled the closed file on this one. Penalty is in the signed final disclosures, but I'm not seeing any language pref or interpreter note anywhere" and "Pulled the closed file. Signed package does show prepay penalty language in the executed disclosures. Looks like signed-docs vs verbal-rep issue" -- and Jasmine Brooks's routing message "Just forwarded the Hassan complaint/disclosure trail to Denise + Keisha + Keith. Holding borrower reply til compliance reviews."
OE 12: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Google review" or "1-star" or "Keisha" (or similar) to surface the 1-star Google review email (sender destiny.pham@gmail.com, subject "New Google review for Keystone Mortgage Partners") that names Keisha Williams and alleges "After weeks of poor communication and delayed callbacks, I was denied and left feeling like I was treated differently than other applicants. It felt discriminatory." This is the canonical second angle Denise flagged and the trigger for the wider Keisha sweep.
OE 13: Enumerate Keisha Williams's recently-closed loans. The agent first resolves Keisha's los_staff id by calling mortgage_los_list_staff (role "loan_officer") and matching on name -- expected id "los_staff_9f0f19845970". Then the agent enumerates her closed loans using mortgage_los_get_pipeline with assigned_to "los_staff_9f0f19845970" and status "closed", paginated via limit/offset until the full set is returned. Note: mortgage_los_search_loans is NOT the right tool here -- it is documented to search by borrower name, loan number, property address, or lender, so a query of "Keisha" or "Williams" would not return Keisha-originated loans by LO assignment. mortgage_los_get_pipeline exposes both assigned_to and status parameters, so it is the correct enumeration path even though its description leads with "active pipeline loans." The full Keisha-originated, status=closed candidate set within the recently-closed window (Oct 2025 -- Apr 2026) is: LN-2025-00440 (closed 2025-10-31), LN-2025-00443 (2025-11-10), LN-2025-00446 (2025-11-04), LN-2025-00451 (2025-11-12), LN-2025-00463 (2025-11-26), LN-2025-00468 (2025-11-26), LN-2025-00478 (2025-12-10), LN-2025-00486 (2025-12-22), LN-2025-00488 (2025-12-20), LN-2025-00490 (2025-12-13), LN-2025-00502 (2026-01-09), LN-2025-00503 (2026-01-14), LN-2025-00517 (2026-02-03), LN-2025-00518 (2026-01-29), LN-2025-00007 (2025-11-14), LN-2026-00199 (2026-03-25), and LN-2026-00297 (2026-03-09). The agent must cover every loan in this set in OE 14-21 and roll the per-loan findings into the consolidated Keisha QC note in OE 22.
OE 14: For each candidate loan returned in OE 13, retrieve the full loan record using mortgage_los_get_loan with the loan_number for that file to capture loan_number, borrower_id, lender_id, closing_date, property_address, and loan_amount. These fields feed the consolidated email findings (loan number, borrower, lender, closing date, what is missing) the prompt requires for each affected file.
OE 15: For each candidate loan from OE 14, pull the full documentation checklist using mortgage_los_get_documents with the loan_id for that file to confirm the checklist has no item documenting language preference, interpreter use, or accommodation -- the prompt explicitly asks the agent to check the document checklist as one of the sources for the language-access gap.
OE 16: For each candidate borrower id surfaced in OE 14, retrieve the borrower record using mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id with that borrower_id to confirm name, email, phone, status, and to confirm no notes field references preferred language. The borrower name from this call drives the contacts and CRM lookups in OE 17 and OE 18.
OE 17: For each candidate borrower from OE 16, look up the borrower's contact record using contacts_search_contacts with a query related to that borrower's name to confirm the contact directory has no field documenting preferred language, interpreter use, or accommodation for this borrower.
OE 18: For each candidate borrower from OE 16, locate the borrower's CRM contact using crm_search_contacts with full_name set to that borrower's full name to capture the CRM contact id needed for both the engagement-note pull in OE 19 and the post-close QC write action in OE 22.
OE 19: For each candidate borrower CRM contact from OE 18, pull all engagement notes using crm_list_engagements with contact_ids set to that borrower's CRM contact id. The agent inspects whether any engagement note references interpreter use, preferred language, or an accommodation conversation. None do.
OE 20: For each candidate borrower from OE 16, search the email trail using search_emails with a query related to that borrower's last name (or full name) to confirm there is no complaint, accommodation request, or language-related correspondence on record. The prompt requires the agent to verify there is "nothing in ... email trail" referencing the language-access concern.
OE 21: For each candidate borrower from OE 16, search Slack mentions using conversations_search_messages with a search_query related to that borrower's name to confirm there is no internal channel discussion of language preference, interpreter use, or accommodation for that borrower.
OE 22: Log two post-close QC notes, one for Hassan and one consolidated for the Keisha sweep. (a) The Hassan note can be written either via crm_create_engagement (engagement_type "NOTE", contact_ids ["crm_crmcontact_8a287efd19fd"], title "Post-close QC: language-access review" or similar) -- which is consistent with where Hassan's existing post-close engagement notes already live -- or via mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_465ef31d23f2", action "post_close_qc_note", and a detail body summarizing the file. Either is a valid reading of "Hassan's record." The body must summarize the Hassan file specifically: loan number LN-2024-00010, closing date 2024-05-08, originating LO Veronica Hayes, lender Caliber Home Loans, signed disclosures contain prepayment penalty language, no documentation of preferred language / interpreter / accommodation found in the loan record, document checklist, borrower contact, CRM engagements, email trail, or Slack history. (b) The Keisha consolidated note is written via crm_create_engagement (engagement_type "NOTE", contact_ids set to Keisha Williams's CRM contact id resolved via crm_search_contacts with full_name "Keisha Williams", title "Post-close QC: Keisha pipeline language-access sweep" or similar). The body walks every Keisha-closed file enumerated in OE 13 -- one line per loan giving loan number, borrower name, lender, closing date, and the language-access finding for that file (clean files included, since the prompt explicitly requires the audit trail to show every file was actually looked at). Most of the per-borrower CRM contacts for the 17 Keisha closed-file borrowers do not exist in crm_contacts.json, so per-borrower engagement notes are not the intended write target -- the consolidated Keisha-anchored note is the audit-trail artifact for the broader sweep. This is two atomic write actions, one rubric per write in the rubric set.
OE 23: Send an email using send_email with sender "keith.langford@keystonemortgage.com", recipients ["denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com"], a subject related to the post-close QC review of language-access documentation across Hassan plus the Keisha sweep (or similar), and a content body that consolidates: (a) the Hassan file findings -- closed 2024-05-08, originated by Veronica Hayes, lender Caliber Home Loans, signed disclosures contain prepayment penalty language, no documentation of preferred language / interpreter / accommodation found anywhere; (b) the 1-star Google review from Destiny Pham naming Keisha as the second angle; (c) the per-loan list from the Keisha sweep with loan number, borrower, lender, closing date, and a one-line note on what is missing; (d) an explicit ask for Denise's direction on how the affected files should be handled from a compliance standpoint going forward.
OE 24: Post a heads-up in the #compliance-alerts channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C004" and a payload briefly noting that a post-close QC review across Hassan and Keisha's recently-closed pipeline turned up multiple files with no language-access documentation in any source, that Denise is reviewing how to handle, and that this is being flagged for visibility before Raymond Chen's next quarterly audit pull.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "Hassan" or "Khalid Hassan" or "prepayment penalty" (or similar) using search_emails to discover the original Khalid Hassan complaint email (sender khalid.hassan82@gmail.com, subject "Question about penalty on my loan"), Jasmine Brooks's routing forward "Fwd: Hassan complaint re prepayment penalty" to Denise + Keisha + Keith, Keith's two prior closed-file findings to Jasmine (CC Denise), and Denise's follow-up question to Keith "Quick check on Hassan file notes" -- this is the entire complaint and review trail across email that the prompt explicitly asks for.
OE 2: Search for the Hassan loan in the LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with a query related to "Hassan" or "Khalid Hassan" (or similar) to surface loan_number LN-2024-00010, id los_loan_465ef31d23f2, borrower_id los_borrower_d8958470fdd4, status closed, closing_date 2024-05-08, lender_id los_lender_723b5e99cb66, assigned_lo los_staff_06b484d01f94, assigned_processor los_staff_0f9038c42665.
OE 3: Retrieve the full Hassan loan record using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2024-00010" to confirm closed status, closing date, lender_id, assigned loan officer id, processor id, loan_amount 120000, purchase_price 150000, rate 7, loan_type conventional, property_address "9832 Tyvola Rd, Charlotte, NC 28277", title_company "Piedmont Settlement Services".
OE 4: Pull the full documentation checklist for the Hassan loan using mortgage_los_get_documents with loan_id "los_loan_465ef31d23f2" to walk every checklist item type. Establish that no checklist item type captures preferred language, interpreter use, or accommodation -- the LOS document checklist schema has no such field, which is the negative finding the prompt is asking for.
OE 5: Retrieve the LOS borrower record for Khalid Hassan using mortgage_los_get_borrower with email "khalid.hassan@gmail.com" (or mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id with borrower_id "los_borrower_d8958470fdd4") to confirm phone (980) 759-7149, address "1315 Johnston Rd, Charlotte, NC 28205", credit_score 715, annual_income 47000, and to check whether the borrower record carries any notes referencing language preference (it does not). Note: the LOS borrower email "khalid.hassan@gmail.com" differs from the borrower's CRM contact / complaint-sender email "khalid.hassan82@gmail.com" -- this is a universe inconsistency, so the email-trail search in OE 1 must use a name-based query rather than relying on the LOS borrower email alone.
OE 6: Look up Hassan's entry in the contacts directory using contacts_search_contacts with query "Khalid Hassan" or "Hassan" to retrieve the borrower contact record and confirm there is no preferred-language, interpreter, or accommodation field populated for this borrower.
OE 7: Locate the Hassan CRM contact using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Khalid Hassan" to capture the borrower's CRM contact id for the engagement-note lookup in OE 8 and the post-close QC note write action in OE 22.
OE 8: List all CRM engagement notes attached to the Hassan borrower contact using crm_list_engagements with contact_ids set to the Hassan CRM contact id from OE 7. The agent is checking whether any prior touchpoint note from origination references preferred language, interpreter use, or an accommodation conversation. None of the existing engagement notes do.
OE 9: Resolve the assigned_lo id "los_staff_06b484d01f94" returned in OE 3 to a staff name using mortgage_los_get_staff with staff_id "los_staff_06b484d01f94" (one-call resolution) or, alternatively, mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false paged until the id matches (Veronica Hayes is terminated and would not appear in the default active-only listing). Expected: Veronica Hayes, role loan_officer, hire_date 2020-01-15, is_active false, termination_date 2025-09-30. This is the answer to the prompt's "the LO situation has changed" hint.
OE 10: Resolve the lender_id "los_lender_723b5e99cb66" returned in OE 3 to a wholesale lender name. There is no get_lender_by_id tool, so the agent pages through mortgage_los_list_lenders (limit/offset only, no name or id filter) and matches the id locally. Expected: Caliber Home Loans, cross-confirmed by the email "Initial Underwriting Conditions - Hassan Loan LN-2024-00010" surfaced in OE 1 with sender broker@caliberhomeloans.com.
OE 11: Search Slack for the Hassan discussion trail using conversations_search_messages with a search_query related to "Hassan" or "prepayment penalty" or "language pref" (or similar). Expected hits include Keith's two #loan-processing (C002) posts -- "Pulled the closed file on this one. Penalty is in the signed final disclosures, but I'm not seeing any language pref or interpreter note anywhere" and "Pulled the closed file. Signed package does show prepay penalty language in the executed disclosures. Looks like signed-docs vs verbal-rep issue" -- and Jasmine Brooks's routing message "Just forwarded the Hassan complaint/disclosure trail to Denise + Keisha + Keith. Holding borrower reply til compliance reviews."
OE 12: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Google review" or "1-star" or "Keisha" (or similar) to surface the 1-star Google review email (sender destiny.pham@gmail.com, subject "New Google review for Keystone Mortgage Partners") that names Keisha Williams and alleges "After weeks of poor communication and delayed callbacks, I was denied and left feeling like I was treated differently than other applicants. It felt discriminatory." This is the canonical second angle Denise flagged and the trigger for the wider Keisha sweep.
OE 13: Enumerate Keisha Williams's recently-closed loans. The agent first resolves Keisha's los_staff id by calling mortgage_los_list_staff (role "loan_officer") and matching on name -- expected id "los_staff_9f0f19845970". Then the agent enumerates her closed loans using mortgage_los_get_pipeline with assigned_to "los_staff_9f0f19845970" and status "closed", paginated via limit/offset until the full set is returned. Note: mortgage_los_search_loans is NOT the right tool here -- it is documented to search by borrower name, loan number, property address, or lender, so a query of "Keisha" or "Williams" would not return Keisha-originated loans by LO assignment. mortgage_los_get_pipeline exposes both assigned_to and status parameters, so it is the correct enumeration path even though its description leads with "active pipeline loans." The full Keisha-originated, status=closed candidate set within the recently-closed window (Oct 2025 -- Apr 2026) is: LN-2025-00440 (closed 2025-10-31), LN-2025-00443 (2025-11-10), LN-2025-00446 (2025-11-04), LN-2025-00451 (2025-11-12), LN-2025-00463 (2025-11-26), LN-2025-00468 (2025-11-26), LN-2025-00478 (2025-12-10), LN-2025-00486 (2025-12-22), LN-2025-00488 (2025-12-20), LN-2025-00490 (2025-12-13), LN-2025-00502 (2026-01-09), LN-2025-00503 (2026-01-14), LN-2025-00517 (2026-02-03), LN-2025-00518 (2026-01-29), LN-2025-00007 (2025-11-14), LN-2026-00199 (2026-03-25), and LN-2026-00297 (2026-03-09). The agent must cover every loan in this set in OE 14-21 and roll the per-loan findings into the consolidated Keisha QC note in OE 22.
OE 14: For each candidate loan returned in OE 13, retrieve the full loan record using mortgage_los_get_loan with the loan_number for that file to capture loan_number, borrower_id, lender_id, closing_date, property_address, and loan_amount. These fields feed the consolidated email findings (loan number, borrower, lender, closing date, what is missing) the prompt requires for each affected file.
OE 15: For each candidate loan from OE 14, pull the full documentation checklist using mortgage_los_get_documents with the loan_id for that file to confirm the checklist has no item documenting language preference, interpreter use, or accommodation -- the prompt explicitly asks the agent to check the document checklist as one of the sources for the language-access gap.
OE 16: For each candidate borrower id surfaced in OE 14, retrieve the borrower record using mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id with that borrower_id to confirm name, email, phone, status, and to confirm no notes field references preferred language. The borrower name from this call drives the contacts and CRM lookups in OE 17 and OE 18.
OE 17: For each candidate borrower from OE 16, look up the borrower's contact record using contacts_search_contacts with a query related to that borrower's name to confirm the contact directory has no field documenting preferred language, interpreter use, or accommodation for this borrower.
OE 18: For each candidate borrower from OE 16, locate the borrower's CRM contact using crm_search_contacts with full_name set to that borrower's full name to capture the CRM contact id needed for both the engagement-note pull in OE 19 and the post-close QC write action in OE 22.
OE 19: For each candidate borrower CRM contact from OE 18, pull all engagement notes using crm_list_engagements with contact_ids set to that borrower's CRM contact id. The agent inspects whether any engagement note references interpreter use, preferred language, or an accommodation conversation. None do.
OE 20: For each candidate borrower from OE 16, search the email trail using search_emails with a query related to that borrower's last name (or full name) to confirm there is no complaint, accommodation request, or language-related correspondence on record. The prompt requires the agent to verify there is "nothing in ... email trail" referencing the language-access concern.
OE 21: For each candidate borrower from OE 16, search Slack mentions using conversations_search_messages with a search_query related to that borrower's name to confirm there is no internal channel discussion of language preference, interpreter use, or accommodation for that borrower.
OE 22: Log two post-close QC notes, one for Hassan and one consolidated for the Keisha sweep. (a) The Hassan note can be written either via crm_create_engagement (engagement_type "NOTE", contact_ids ["crm_crmcontact_8a287efd19fd"], title "Post-close QC: language-access review" or similar) -- which is consistent with where Hassan's existing post-close engagement notes already live -- or via mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_465ef31d23f2", action "post_close_qc_note", and a detail body summarizing the file. Either is a valid reading of "Hassan's record." The body must summarize the Hassan file specifically: loan number LN-2024-00010, closing date 2024-05-08, originating LO Veronica Hayes, lender Caliber Home Loans, signed disclosures contain prepayment penalty language, no documentation of preferred language / interpreter / accommodation found in the loan record, document checklist, borrower contact, CRM engagements, email trail, or Slack history. (b) The Keisha consolidated note is written via crm_create_engagement (engagement_type "NOTE", contact_ids set to Keisha Williams's CRM contact id resolved via crm_search_contacts with full_name "Keisha Williams", title "Post-close QC: Keisha pipeline language-access sweep" or similar). The body walks every Keisha-closed file enumerated in OE 13 -- one line per loan giving loan number, borrower name, lender, closing date, and the language-access finding for that file (clean files included, since the prompt explicitly requires the audit trail to show every file was actually looked at). Most of the per-borrower CRM contacts for the 17 Keisha closed-file borrowers do not exist in crm_contacts.json, so per-borrower engagement notes are not the intended write target -- the consolidated Keisha-anchored note is the audit-trail artifact for the broader sweep. This is two atomic write actions, one rubric per write in the rubric set.
OE 23: Send an email using send_email with sender "keith.langford@keystonemortgage.com", recipients ["denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com"], a subject related to the post-close QC review of language-access documentation across Hassan plus the Keisha sweep (or similar), and a content body that consolidates: (a) the Hassan file findings -- closed 2024-05-08, originated by Veronica Hayes, lender Caliber Home Loans, signed disclosures contain prepayment penalty language, no documentation of preferred language / interpreter / accommodation found anywhere; (b) the 1-star Google review from Destiny Pham naming Keisha as the second angle; (c) the per-loan list from the Keisha sweep with loan number, borrower, lender, closing date, and a one-line note on what is missing; (d) an explicit ask for Denise's direction on how the affected files should be handled from a compliance standpoint going forward.
OE 24: Post a heads-up in the #compliance-alerts channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C004" and a payload briefly noting that a post-close QC review across Hassan and Keisha's recently-closed pipeline turned up multiple files with no language-access documentation in any source, that Denise is reviewing how to handle, and that this is being flagged for visibility before Raymond Chen's next quarterly audit pull.

outcome

14 The content of the email to [denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com](mailto:denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) identifies the originating loan officer on the Hassan file as Veronica Hayes.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email content explicitly states 'Veronica Hayes as assigned LO' for the Hassan loan, satisfying the requirement to identify the originating loan officer.
✓Run #2The email content explicitly states 'LO of record (LOS): Veronica Hayes (terminated 09/30/2025)' identifying Veronica Hayes as the originating loan officer on the Hassan file.
✓Run #3The email content explicitly states 'LOS assigned LO on closest matching record (LN-2024-00010): Veronica Hayes (NMLS NC-208311, terminated 09/30/2025)', identifying Veronica Hayes as the originating loan officer.
✓Run #4The email content explicitly states 'Originating LO: Veronica Hayes (terminated 09/30/2025)' in the Hassan file section.
